title: Administrative Assistant
description:
Job Summary

We are seeking a detail-oriented Application Assessment Officer to serve as the primary point of contact for applicants to our scheme. This role involves comprehensive application processing from initial review through to final approval and post-approval activities, ensuring a smooth and efficient experience for all applicants.

Responsibilities

Application Processing and Review

  • Conduct thorough initial assessments of incoming applications to verify completeness and compliance with scheme requirements
  • Identify and resolve missing documentation or information gaps to maintain application quality standards
  • Maintain accurate and up-to-date records of application status and progress using established tracking systems

Stakeholder Communication and Coordination

  • Communicate clearly and professionally with applicants to clarify requirements, request additional information, and provide timely updates on application status
  • Coordinate effectively with internal teams and external stakeholders to facilitate a seamless application review process
  • Respond promptly and professionally to applicant enquiries throughout the assessment period to ensure positive applicant experience

Decision Communication and Follow-up

  • Notify applicants of assessment outcomes, delivering clear information on next steps for both successful and unsuccessful applications
  • Process acceptance forms from successful applicants and verify receipt of all required documentation
  • Collect and review content materials from successful applicants for website publication, ensuring accuracy and completeness

Required competencies and certifications

  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and database management systems to manage application data efficiently
  • Ability to maintain confidentiality and handle sensitive information appropriately to protect applicant privacy

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • At least 2-3 years of experience in administrative roles involving application processing or customer service
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills to interact professionally with diverse stakeholders
  • Excellent attention to detail and accuracy in document review and data entry to ensure data integrity
  • Strong organisational skills to manage multiple applications simultaneously and meet deadlines
  • Customer service orientation demonstrating patience and empathy when dealing with applicants
  • Ability to work independently while maintaining regular communication with supervisors
  • Adaptability to changing processes and requirements to support continuous improvement
  • Understanding of government processes and procedures advantageous for navigating scheme requirements
  • Experience with content management systems preferred to support website publication tasks


Location: City Hall / Clarke Quay
Mondays to Fridays office hours
Duration: 3 Months (extendable)

EA License No: 96C4864
Reg. No.: R24121728 EUNICE WOO JING QI
